methods.description: Procedure for processing bulk sediment samples to isolate and sort Carychium shells for analysis.

steps.description: Two 7.5 l bulk samples from the middle of the freshwater layer were sorted by hand,
washed through a graded series of sieves,
and allowed to dry for further processing.
Thirty-two Carychium shells were culled from a mixture of other molluscs and debris under a stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Cull target shells from dried sample using stereomicroscope

methods.description: Assessment of qualitative morphological features and internal structures of Carychium shells using visual inspection and micro-CT imaging.

steps.description: Several qualitative aspects of shell morphology are addressed including peristome shape; whorl profile (whorl convexity); teleoconch sculpture;
steps.searchable_summary: Assess qualitative shell morphology features (peristome shape, whorl profile, sculpture)

steps.description: development of apertural barriers visible in frontal view, including the presence of a deeply immersed denticle/lamella on the parieto-columellar region of the aperture;
steps.searchable_summary: Assess apertural barriers in frontal view (including parieto-columellar denticle/lamella)

steps.description: development of the columellar lamella as discernable in the micro-CT scans of the ventral, dorsal, side-left, side-right and umbilical perspectives of the adult shell.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess columellar lamella development using micro-CT scans from multiple perspectives (ventral, dorsal, side-left, side-right, umbilical)
